refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load→travel→dump cycle.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR specifies a single functional obligation: to support achieving a defined cycle time target for a specific operational cycle. It is already focused on what the system must do (support achieving the cycle time target) without specifying how, thus it is solution-neutral and atomic.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the cycle time for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, and verify that the 95th percentile (p95) cycle time is less than or equal to the customer-defined target (CT_target).
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load→travel→dump cycle.

candidate_dps: Optimize load handling mechanism to minimize load time
Optimize travel path and speed to minimize travel time
Optimize dump mechanism to minimize dump time
Integrate cycle time monitoring and adaptive control to dynamically meet cycle time targets
Balance load, travel, and dump operations to achieve overall cycle time target
selected_dp.selected_dp: Balance load, travel, and dump operations to achieve overall cycle time target

potential_sources: Interdependence between load, travel, and dump times affecting total cycle time
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP explicitly considers balancing the three sub-operations to meet the overall cycle time target, promoting independence by treating each sub-operation as a controllable parameter within the overall cycle.

assumptions_risks: Cycle time can be decomposed into load, travel, and dump components with measurable contributions
Operational policies and safety plans do not impose constraints that prevent optimizing any of the sub-operations
Environmental and site conditions remain within agreed parameters to ensure cycle time targets are achievable
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly decomposing the FR into sub-FRs for load, travel, and dump cycle times to enable more granular DP selection and clearer independence analysis.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the overall system logic responsible for balancing load, travel, and dump operations to meet the cycle time target.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends operational status and performance data to external monitoring systems.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands and task assignments to coordinate load, travel, and dump operations.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates communication between NGHE Solution and other modules such as Vehicle and Teleoperation.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as the DP involves system-wide operational balancing best managed here; minimal diffusion as coordination logic is centralized.
Vehicle handles physical operations but balancing cycle time requires coordination beyond vehicle scope, causing diffusion.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control, not on balancing operational cycle times, leading to poor containment and high diffusion.
Safety Services focus on safety alerts and monitoring, unrelated to operational balancing, resulting in very weak containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the DP because it centrally manages the coordination and balancing of load, travel, and dump operations to meet cycle time targets, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential complexity within NGHE Solution may grow as it handles multiple operational aspects, risking reduced modularity.
Interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation must be well-defined to avoid hidden dependencies and diffusion.
If operational balancing logic is not well encapsulated, diffusion into Vehicle or Teleoperation modules may increase.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment risks materialize, consider decomposing the DP into sub-DPs focused on load balancing and travel coordination separately, allowing more specific module boundaries such as Vehicle for travel operations and NGHE Solution for overall coordination.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by focusing on balancing load, travel, and dump operations to meet the cycle time target, which directly supports achieving the customer-defined cycle time target as specified.

diffusion_risks: Interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may cause diffusion if coordination logic is not well encapsulated within the NGHE Solution module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and minimal diffusion risk, as it centrally manages the balancing logic. Other modules have lower containment scores and higher diffusion, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the primary IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through measuring the cycle time p95 against the CT_target under agreed duty cycle and site conditions, as well as ensuring compliance with worksite safety plans and operational policies.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution module is clear, with well-defined interfaces for telemetry, tasking, and network communication supporting coordination and monitoring.

recommended_revisions: Monitor complexity growth within the NGHE Solution module to maintain modularity and consider decomposing the DP into sub-DPs if diffusion risks increase.
Ensure interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules are explicitly defined and managed to prevent hidden dependencies and diffusion.
If operational balancing logic begins to diffuse into other modules, revise the architecture to separate load balancing and travel coordination into distinct sub-DPs with clearer module boundaries.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear logical mapping, manageable diffusion risks, and feasible verification methods. The NGHE Solution module demonstrates high cohesion and minimal diffusion risk, with well-defined interfaces and clear allocation. Although there are recommendations to monitor complexity and interface management, these are forward-looking and do not necessitate further decomposition at this stage. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear and stable architecture without unnecessary complexity is prudent.

assumptions: The current DP sufficiently balances load, travel, and dump operations to meet cycle time targets under expected duty cycles and site conditions.
Interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules remain well-managed and do not introduce hidden dependencies.
Verification methods for cycle time and safety compliance are reliable and repeatable.
open_risks: Potential complexity growth within the NGHE Solution module leading to diffusion if not monitored.
Interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may cause diffusion if coordination logic is not well encapsulated.
evidence_needed: Ongoing monitoring data of module complexity and interface stability.
Verification results confirming cycle time p95 meets CT_target under operational conditions.
Audit of interface definitions and management practices with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
